title: Aussie Broadband: $25.20 Cashback for nbn FTTP/HFC 500/50 $50/Month for 3 Months (New Customers & Stay 90 Days) @ TopCashback AU
body:
Deal: Aussie Broadband: $25.20 Cashback for nbn FTTP/HFC 500/50 $50/Month for 3 Months (New Customers & Stay 90 Days) @ TopCashback AU

[HD9990] new customers
what about previous users left them years ago?
[hamza23] It's in the post:
A ‘new customer/service’ is defined as an individual who has not had an active broadband or mobile service with Aussie Broadband in the last six months or hasn’t been an authorised contact, residing at the same address as the primary contact, on an active broadband or mobile service with Aussie Broadband in the last six months.
[HD9990] thanks mate, this looks pretty good then.
are they as good as they were couple years ago? I had a rough memory their service/reputation has gone down quite a bit recently?
[scalebearer] ($50 x 3 - $25.20) / 3 = $41.60 per month if you decide to port out after just 3 months and risk the systems thinking you ported out just under 90 days, depending on how they count it.
($50 x 3 + $99 - $25.20) / 4 = $55.95 if you decide to play it safe and stay for 4 months.
The regular price for 500/50 is $99, which is really really painful if you don't make full use of the 500 down, 50 up bandwidth.
Most of their lower priced plans are also very uncompetitively priced at the undiscounted rates, so you're not saving that much even if you switch to a cheaper plan for that 4th month.
[Twix] Aussie BB does pro-rata refunds. Stay a couple extra days if you want to play it safe. You don't have to stay a whole extra month.
[ltzJack] If you connect in July or August you'll have 92 days for the first 3 months, enough to meet the 90-day criteria if you leave on the last date in your 3rd billing cycle.
[placard] Just changed from Aussie to Leaptel due to Aussie jacking up their prices.
Leaptel is the same quality service but a fair bit cheaper.
[Tuttle] Why negged? 3 months only is pretty weak
[cheap4321] When does the service have to start? I signed up with FIRSTIN50 in mid June for end of August start with no cashback.
Thinking about contacting cs to get this one.
What’s the estimated payable date for those who have tracked?
[ltzJack] No mention of start date from TCB, I scheduled the start date to be 28th Sept and it tracked in 5 minutes, so I think it was your TCB not tracking properly, raise a missing cashback claim if you want.
[Blitzfx] How far ahead are you allowed to delay connection?
[ltzJack] 3 months online, call ABB customer support to schedule for an even later date
[VladImpaler] Thanks for the tip, I didn’t want to switch until my last Superloop cashback was confirmed but hopefully that won't be an issue with such an extended future connection date.
The Topcashback confirmation email has come through, but still waiting on Aussie Broadband email confirmation.
[Callandor] Wouldn't using the discount code invalidate the cashback?
[ltzJack] As mentioned in the first sentence of my post:
Just found TCB has added FIRSTIN50 as its coupon code so it is valid for cashback for this deal
[Genuine guy] Is it applicable to the Opticomm connections too? Thanks.
[ganjadeals] They still don't do 500/50 on Opticomm. You're better off with other providers who actively adapt NBN speed upgrades and provide better speeds on top on good customer service.
[BargainsGrabber] The only downside I could see from this deal is that you need to stay for 90 days. The discount ends after 3 months and unsure if you need to do the 4th month
[Bennie] MORE NBN have same deal via commbank. 500/50 for $75 for 36 months.
I did speed test and it came as aussie broadband. Are they the same company?
[Twix] More does use Aussie BB for their nbn services.
More and Aussie BB are not the same company.
[li1123k] Isn't Dodo nbn $120 TCB deal have a better profit margin and you only need to stay with them for 2months (or 3 just be safe)? I signed up today and it got tracked in 5 minutes.
[ltzJack] You can sign up for both as ABB allows you to schedule for 3 months, like I did today :)
[ElectricMallet] Dodo online ordering is down for maintenance… I was about to squeeze in a last minute sign up, but no deal :(
[Make it so] I don't normally like churning too fast - makes me feel guilty. But Spintel had promised me $50 for 6 months, then increased the price anyway. Hopefully it finishes before the end of the day, or else Spintel will charge me an extra month.
[Kustard King] I think I'm just about done with TCB - the last 2 buys did not track (despite using the app) and then it's waiting months for the claim to get approved.
[ltzJack] I just checked on TCB and ABB, both still available, can you provide a screenshot?
[randomsquat] I've tried clicking on it, which brings me to the website but it's only applying BEST120 instead…
[ltzJack] That's normal, you should remove that coupon and enter FIRSTIN50.
If on mobile, after adding to cart, click the up arrow on YOUR ORDER BREAKDOWN box, then you can apply the code.
[randomsquat] @ltzJack: Right, got it. Thank you!
BTW Promo applied: FIRSTIN50 -$45/monthly for first 3 months…
…I wonder if this works out to be $50 for the first month, but $54 thereafter due to the price hike.
[ltzJack] @randomsquat: No, in the confirmation email you'll get:
Promotion/discount $50 a month plan price for 3 months
[randomsquat] @ltzJack: Sounds good, will await email.
CB already tracked.
[randomsquat] I've tried clicking on it, which brings me to the website but it's only applying BEST120 instead…
[ruztynail] whats wrong using amaysim nbn.. its $50 /mth for 500mbps for 6 montsh? isnt that much better?
